name: VisGraph
description:
Interactive RDF/ontology knowledge graph editor — ABox authoring, OWL-RL reasoning, layout, and export. All client-side, no backend.

Architecture for AI agents: The app has two coupled layers. (1) N3 RDF store — the source of truth for all triples. Tools like addNode/addLink write triples here first. (2) Reactodia canvas — a visual model that mirrors a subset of the store as draggable node cards (subjects with annotations) and arrows (object-property triples). Canvas nodes are NOT created automatically when triples are added — you must call addNode to place a subject on canvas. After adding triples, the canvas refreshes links automatically. Nodes start collapsed; call expandNode or expandAll to reveal annotation property cards. OWL-RL reasoning writes inferred triples back to the store and refreshes the canvas.

Recommended workflow: loadOntology (TBox) → addNode ×N (ABox individuals) → addLink ×N (object properties) → runLayout → expandAll → runReasoning → fitCanvas → exportImage(svg) → exportGraph(turtle).

tools.name: loadRdf
tools.description: Load RDF instance data (ABox) into the canvas from a URL or a Turtle string. Creates canvas nodes for each subject.

tools.name: loadOntology
tools.description: Load an ontology (TBox) by URL. Feeds rdf:type autocomplete, domain/range hints, and OWL-RL reasoning. Does NOT add canvas nodes.

tools.name: queryGraph
tools.description: Run a SPARQL SELECT query against the in-memory RDF store. Returns rows as JSON.

tools.name: exportGraph
tools.description: Export the current RDF graph as Turtle, JSON-LD, or RDF-XML.

tools.name: exportImage
tools.description: Export the canvas diagram as SVG text (default, token-efficient) or PNG data URI. Call fitCanvas first to ensure all nodes are visible.

tools.name: addNode
tools.description: Create an ABox individual (subject node) on the canvas. A node is an RDF resource — it has an IRI, an optional rdf:type pointing to a class, and annotation properties (rdfs:label, etc.) shown as a card on the canvas.

typeIri.description: IRI of the rdf:type class (e.g. foaf:Person)

tools.name: removeNode
tools.description: Remove a canvas node (ABox individual) and all its RDF triples. Both the canvas element and all quads with this IRI as subject are deleted.

tools.name: expandNode
tools.description: Expand a canvas node to reveal its annotation properties (rdfs:label, rdf:type, datatype values). Nodes start collapsed — call this to make the property card visible. Pass expand=false to collapse.

tools.name: expandAll
tools.description: Expand all canvas nodes to reveal their annotation property cards.

tools.name: getNodes
tools.description: Return ABox individuals currently on the canvas with their IRI, label, and rdf:type(s). Optionally filter by type IRI or label substring.

tools.name: addLink
tools.description: Add a directed object-property triple (edge) between two canvas nodes: subjectIri --predicateIri--> objectIri. The edge appears on canvas immediately. Both subject and object must already be canvas nodes.

predicateIri.description: IRI of the object property (e.g. foaf:knows)

tools.name: removeLink
tools.description: Remove an object-property triple (edge) between two nodes.

tools.name: getLinks
tools.description: Return object-property triples (edges) currently in the RDF store. Filter by subject, predicate, or object IRI.

tools.name: searchEntities
tools.description: Search ABox individuals and ontology classes by label or IRI substring. Returns IRI + label pairs. Use before addNode/addLink to resolve the correct full IRI.

tools.name: autocomplete
tools.description: Autocomplete entity IRIs by partial label or IRI text. Use before authoring to get the exact IRI for a known entity.

tools.name: runLayout
tools.description: Apply a layout algorithm to reposition canvas nodes.

tools.name: focusNode
tools.description: Pan and zoom the canvas viewport to centre on a specific node by IRI.

tools.name: fitCanvas
tools.description: Zoom the canvas viewport to fit all nodes. Call before exportImage to ensure all nodes are visible.

tools.name: runReasoning
tools.description: Run OWL-RL inference over the loaded graph and refresh the canvas with inferred types and properties. Returns count of new inferred triples. Pass clearBefore=true to discard previous inferences first.

tools.name: clearInferred
tools.description: Remove all OWL-RL derived (inferred) triples from the graph.

tools.name: getCapabilities
tools.description: Return available layout algorithms, export formats, and loaded ontologies.

tools.name: getGraphState
tools.description: Return a summary of the current canvas: node count, link count, and per-node IRI/label/types. Use to verify canvas state before or after mutations.
